title: Minuartia confusa
intro: Minuartia confusa, commonly known as Alpine Rock-cress, is a small, perennial plant belonging to the family Caryophyllaceae, characterized by its delicate white or pink flowers. The plant has been traditionally used in herbal medicine to treat various health conditions, including inflammation, fever, and respiratory issues, due to its potential anti-inflammatory and expectorant properties. Bioactive constituents of Minuartia confusa include flavonoids, such as kaempferol and quercetin, as well as phenolic acids, which contribute to its medicinal properties. Alpine Rock-cress is often used to make teas, infusions, and tinctures, which are commonly consumed to aid in the relief of respiratory issues and to promote overall well-being.
